title: Washington, D.C. 3-Day City Break
metaTitle: Washington, D.C. 3-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Washington, D.C., United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including International Spy Museum and Washington Monument (timed entry). Prices from $622 per person (estimate).
highlights: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Washington, D.C., United States — Humid subtropical climate: hot, humid summers (highs 30-32°C with high humidity) and cold winters (highs 5-7°C, occasional snow), with a famously beautiful, brief cherry blossom season in late March/early April. Late March to April for the National Cherry Blossom Festival and mild highs (16-20°C), and September-October for comfortable fall weather; summer (Jun-Aug) is hot, humid and crowded with school groups. This itinerary blends International Spy Museum, Washington Monument (timed entry), Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including International Spy Museum, Washington Monument (timed entry), Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Washington, D.C.
itinerary.description: Land at Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to International Spy Museum — Interactive exhibits on the history of espionage. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Washington, D.C.: Washington Monument (timed entry) & Newseum successor exhibits / The Museum of the Bible
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Washington Monument (timed entry) — Obelisk memorial with an observation deck, free tickets timed and limited. In the afternoon, continue to Newseum successor exhibits / The Museum of the Bible — Private museum near the National Mall. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Newseum successor exhibits / The Museum of the Bible

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to National Cathedral tower tour or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to IAD for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book free timed-entry passes for popular museums (like the National Museum of African American History and Culture) online in advance.
Visit the Tidal Basin cherry blossoms at sunrise during peak bloom (typically late March-early April) to beat both crowds and heat.
The National Mall is larger than it looks on a map — plan a Metro stop strategy rather than trying to walk the whole span in one go.
White House tours require advance requests through a member of Congress or your embassy, often weeks ahead.
Most Smithsonian museums are free — no need to prioritize paid attractions over these first-rate collections.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Comfortable walking shoes — the National Mall spans nearly 2 miles end to end
Layers for humid summers and cold winters
A light jacket for spring cherry blossom season evenings
A photo ID, useful for security checks at federal buildings
thingsToDo: Newseum successor exhibits / The Museum of the Bible
Bureau of Engraving and Printing tour
Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture
Segway or bike tours of the National Mall
Rock climbing/hiking day trips to nearby Great Falls Park
National Zoo (part of the Smithsonian, free entry)
thingsToAvoid: Bringing large bags or prohibited items to the Capitol, White House area or Smithsonian museums — security screening is strict.
Assuming spontaneous cherry blossom timing — peak bloom dates shift each year and are worth checking closer to travel.
Walking the entire Mall without a rest/Metro strategy — distances between the Capitol and Lincoln Memorial are longer than they appear.
Visiting in high summer without sun/heat protection — humidity makes the Mall's open spaces feel hotter than the temperature suggests.
Photographing security personnel or restricted federal buildings without checking posted rules.
topRestaurants: Ben's Chili Bowl — American Diner, DC institution ($)
Old Ebbitt Grill — American, historic tavern ($$$)
The Diner, Adams Morgan — American Diner, 24-hour ($)
nightlife: U Street Corridor jazz clubs (including historic Bohemian Caverns legacy venues)
instagramSpots: Tidal Basin cherry blossoms with the Jefferson Memorial
National Mall from the Washington Monument
familyActivities: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum

adventureActivities: Segway or bike tours of the National Mall
Rock climbing/hiking day trips to nearby Great Falls Park
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at The Hay-Adams overlooking the White House
Fine dining at Rasika or Le Diplomate
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
Cherry blossom walk along the Tidal Basin (late March-early April)
Private evening monuments tour by pedicab
budgetExperiences: Free entry to all Smithsonian museums on the National Mall
Free monuments and memorials self-guided walk
Free Bureau of Engraving and Printing tour (advance ticket)

festivalCalendar.name: Fourth of July celebrations on the National Mall

localEtiquette: Security screening (airport-style) is required to enter the Capitol, White House (advance request needed) and many federal buildings — arrive early and carry ID.
Dress respectfully and keep quiet at war memorials such as the Vietnam Veterans Memorial and Arlington National Cemetery.
Photography is welcomed at most monuments, but be mindful of ongoing ceremonies (e.g., the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier changing of the guard).
localTransportGuide: The Washington Metro subway system covers the city and suburbs efficiently and connects directly to both Reagan National and (via bus/rail link) Dulles airports; the National Mall area is very walkable once you arrive. Capital Bikeshare and rideshare apps fill in the gaps.
bestTimeToVisit: Late March to April for the National Cherry Blossom Festival and mild highs (16-20°C), and September-October for comfortable fall weather; summer (Jun-Aug) is hot, humid and crowded with school groups.
weather: Humid subtropical climate: hot, humid summers (highs 30-32°C with high humidity) and cold winters (highs 5-7°C, occasional snow), with a famously beautiful, brief cherry blossom season in late March/early April.
visaInformation: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview — a frequent stop for Indian diplomatic, business and diaspora travelers given the concentration of embassies and federal institutions.
averageFlightTime: ~14.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Washington, D.C.?
faqs.answer: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview — a frequent stop for Indian diplomatic, business and diaspora travelers given the concentration of embassies and federal institutions.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Washington, D.C.?
faqs.answer: Late March to April for the National Cherry Blossom Festival and mild highs (16-20°C), and September-October for comfortable fall weather; summer (Jun-Aug) is hot, humid and crowded with school groups. Humid subtropical climate: hot, humid summers (highs 30-32°C with high humidity) and cold winters (highs 5-7°C, occasional snow), with a famously beautiful, brief cherry blossom season in late March/early April.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Washington, D.C. safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Washington, D.C. is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Washington, D.C.?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum.

reviews.author: Grace Fernandez
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Larsen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with International Spy Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Daniel Tanaka
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to International Spy Museum and Lincoln Memorial. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Chen Santos
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Tidal Basin cherry blossom path in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Novak
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Lincoln Memorial alone was worth the trip, but combined with Bureau of Engraving and Printing tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Olivia Johnson
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Our trip to Washington, D.C. was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Smith
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Washington Monument (timed entry) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Anna Andersen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Washington, D.C. is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Washington Monument (timed entry). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
